publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-10-10 23:00:18 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Personal correspondence; bills, receipts and accounts for merchandise and services. Includes an executive order issued by Governor Throop for the transfer of prisoners from Auburn to Sing Sing, the former prison being overcrowded.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The Enos Thompson Throop papers contain intra-family correspondence about business, weddings, deaths, and travel; receipts from tradesmen, hardware, agricultural implements, repair, newspaper, general stores, and clothing; a farm labourers accounts, 1834-1838, including prices for produce and purchases. The collection ncludes a letter to State Assemblyman questioning his campaign promise of a bill to place the County court house in Auburn, 1807, and an executive order issued by Governor Throop for the transfer of prisoners from Auburn to Sing Sing, the former prison being overcrowded, February 11, 1830.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Enos Thompson Throop. Papers, #1157. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.
